ACT — Health policy (current, from act.org.nz/health)

Our Values: New Zealand's health system should deliver timely, high-quality care based on individual need, regardless of background. We support a competitive, innovative health sector where public and private providers work together to deliver the best results for patients. Our focus is on cutting waste, improving accountability, and ensuring every taxpayer dollar is spent where it makes the most difference.

Achievements ACT cites: Abolishing the Māori Health Authority (ending what ACT calls race-based division, a single accountable organisation). Removing race-based provisions from the Pae Ora Act, replacing them with objectives to deliver timely services for all patients based on need. Introducing measurable, trackable targets for Health NZ. Partnering with the private sector — Health NZ offering private hospitals long-term contracts for elective surgeries to increase capacity and reduce wait times. Focusing health funding on frontline services rather than management.

Next goals: Expand public–private partnerships — ACT wants the Government to be primarily a purchaser, not a provider, of health services, expanding contracts with private providers for diagnostics, specialist care and elective procedures so patients are treated faster. Remove remaining race-based health policies, delivering services on the basis of patient need and value for money without preference based on ethnicity. Increase transparency — publishing regular detailed performance reports for hospitals and health services.
